ground_truth.title: Best live TV streaming service: The top 5 picks for entertainment, sports, and more
ground_truth.author: Jared Newman and Sam Singleton
ground_truth.main_content:
When you’re finally ready to cut the cord, shopping for a live TV streaming service is an obvious starting point. They offer many of the same channels you had with cable or satellite TV, but deliver them over the internet to your smart TV or streaming device. All that said, live TV streaming services have been getting more and more expensive, and in some ways they won’t be exactly like what you had with cable or satellite. Our top picks will help you avoid overpaying and get the TV channels you care about the most.

YouTube TV – Best TV streaming service overall
Who should use YouTube TV?
If you want to cut the cable cord without overthinking it, YouTube TV is the easiest starting point to recommend. It’s the best drop-in replacement for cable or satellite TV. It’s not exactly cheap at $83 per month, but it offers a broad mix of the local stations, live news, national sports, and entertainment channels you’d get with a typical pay TV package, almost certainly at a lower price. Its interface is easy to comprehend, its cloud DVR lets you record an unlimited number of programs for up to nine months, and you can even bundle NFL Sunday Ticket at a discount.
YouTube TV: Further considerations
Other services may offer a better value—either by bundling other services or omitting popular channels—or by delivering more regional sports coverage. Also, persistent price hikes mean that YouTube TV is no longer the leader on value that it once was. Another thing to note is that the service streams at up to 720p resolutions for most cable channels, with 1080p support for on-demand recordings. There is also wide device support with Roku, Apple TV, most Fire TV devices, and most smart TVs from 2021 onwards.

Hulu + Live TV – Best TV streaming service, runner-up
Who should use Hulu + Live TV?
The main thing that distinguishes Hulu + Live TV from YouTube TV is its inclusion of the full Disney bundle, so you get Hulu’s on-demand catalog, Disney+ (with ads, unless you were grandfathered in), and ESPN+ in the $90-per-month base package. Hulu’s cloud DVR service lets you save an unlimited number of shows for up to nine months, and while the interface can be busy, it ties everything together in a way that encourages discovery.
Hulu + Live TV: Further considerations
Hulu’s live TV package makes a lot of sense if you’re paying for any of Disney’s other streaming services. Disney+ and Hulu each cost $10 per month on their own (or $11 per month together), while ESPN+ is even pricier at $12 per month (also available with Disney+ and Hulu for $17 per month). With Hulu + Live TV, you get all those services at no extra cost.

DirecTV Stream – Best TV streaming service for sports fans
Who should use DirecTV Stream?
DirecTV Stream is the best live TV streaming service for sports fans, especially those who want to watch regional sports networks. It’s the only major live TV streaming service that still carries Bally Sports, which holds the rights to many MLB, NBA, and NHL teams. It also carries the YES Network for New York Yankees and Brooklyn Nets games, and NESN for Boston Red Sox and Bruins games.
DirecTV Stream: Further considerations
DirecTV Stream is also the only live TV streaming service that offers a full-fledged cable-like experience, with a traditional grid guide and the ability to watch on multiple TVs at once without any extra hardware. It’s also the only service that offers 4K streaming for some live sports and on-demand content. However, it’s also the most expensive live TV streaming service, with plans starting at $84.99 per month and going up to $154.99 per month for the Premier package.

Sling TV – Best TV streaming service for budget-minded cord-cutters
Who should use Sling TV?
Sling TV is the best live TV streaming service for budget-minded cord-cutters. It’s the only major live TV streaming service that doesn’t require a contract, and it offers a variety of packages that start at just $40 per month. Sling TV also offers a variety of add-ons, so you can customize your package to include the channels you want without paying for channels you don’t.
Sling TV: Further considerations
Sling TV is a great option for cord-cutters who want to save money, but it’s not for everyone. It doesn’t offer local channels in all markets, and it doesn’t offer as many channels as some of the other services. It also doesn’t offer a cloud DVR, so you can’t record shows to watch later. However, Sling TV does offer a variety of on-demand content, and it’s available on a wide variety of devices.

FuboTV – Best TV streaming service for international sports
Who should use FuboTV?
FuboTV is the best live TV streaming service for international sports fans. It offers a wide variety of international sports channels, including beIN Sports, ESPN Deportes, and Fox Deportes. It also offers a variety of other channels, including local channels, news channels, and entertainment channels.
FuboTV: Further considerations
FuboTV is a great option for international sports fans, but it’s not for everyone. It’s one of the more expensive live TV streaming services, with plans starting at $79.99 per month. It also doesn’t offer as many channels as some of the other services, and it doesn’t offer a cloud DVR. However, FuboTV does offer a variety of on-demand content, and it’s available on a wide variety of devices.

If you want to cut the cable cord without overthinking it, YouTube TV is the easiest starting point to recommend.
